methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vyoma is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised €15M+. The valuation universe spans 13 comparable companies across public and private markets: 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Peer-median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort—only 2 adjacent-stage peers identified against a threshold of 3+ required datapoints. Public-comp anchoring using the 6-ticker Space & Aerospace median P/S of 25.8x could not proceed without Vyoma's disclosed revenue, which is not on file. Private companies at this stage typically do not publish revenue metrics, precluding a reliable top-down multiple application.

The analysis reveals a constrained dataset. While 13 total sector comps exist, the high heterogeneity within Space & Aerospace—spanning launch services, satellite operators, on-orbit services, and subsystems—and Vyoma's specific position within that landscape remain underspecified in available data. A defensible valuation range cannot be triangulated without either (1) Vyoma's revenue or operating metrics, (2) transaction multiples from recent Series A rounds in adjacent aerospace segments, or (3) clearer stage-and-segment alignment within the peer set. Further due diligence on comparable funding rounds and Vyoma's revenue trajectory is required before establishing a reliable valuation estimate.
